payload.title: Agents need a loadable Narada CLI embodiment instead of PATH repair by memory
payload.summary: During User Site inbox processing, narada-andrey.architect fell back to hardcoded WSL Node bin paths to run the Narada CLI. The immediate rule is correct: agents should use the Narada shim inside the target embodiment, or report delegated CLI embodiment not loadable with the exact doctor/preflight repair command.
payload.observation: The Narada proper shim at node_modules/.bin/narada works under the intended login-shell WSL embodiment, but fails under non-login WSL with exec: node: not found. The User Site doctor also reports no Site-local delegated Narada CLI embodiment configured and says to run narada inbox doctor before cross-environment inbox submission when runtime posture is ambiguous.
evidence: Non-login WSL call: ./node_modules/.bin/narada failed with 'exec: node: not found'.
Login-shell WSL call: ./node_modules/.bin/narada inbox doctor --cwd /mnt/c/Users/Andrey/Narada --format json succeeded.

Doctor reported delegated_cli_embodiment.configured=false for the User Site and canonical_inbox_commands_available=false because /mnt/c/Users/Andrey/Narada/packages/layers/cli/dist/main.js is missing.
Doctor repair guidance included: run pnpm --filter @narada2/cli build when a local repo CLI build is expected.
payload.requested_handling: Treat this as ergonomics/product work: make the delegated CLI embodiment explicit, make doctor/preflight repair instructions easy to surface, and discourage hand-assembled Node PATH commands in agent-facing guidance.
